channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What I learned by vibe-coding my own word processor
item.description: Hello again, and welcome back to Fast Company’s Plugged In. Before we go any further, an invitation: On Thursday, April 23, at 1 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: They bought property in the metaverse. Then it collapsed
item.description: Five years ago, tech angel investor Chris Adamo and a few friends jumped on a burgeoning trend in the digital asset world: they used a virtual real estate broker to buy 23 parcels of property in a metaverse called The Sandbox.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: When cloud giants neglect resilience
item.description: In a recent article chronicling the history of Microsoft Azure and its intensifying woes, we see a narrative that has been building throughout the industry for years.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why AI is the ultimate accelerator for creativity
item.description: Have you noticed that in the current discourse around artificial intelligence, the narrative often slips into one of two extremes? There is either a techno-utopian dream of total automation or a dystopian nightmare where human agency is erased.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Nixon, Fast Company

item.title: Meta employee accused of accessing private images
item.description: A former Meta employee is accused of accessing around 30,000 private Facebook photos by allegedly bypassing internal security systems. What to know now.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI isn’t built for all languages and cultures. There’s a push to fix that
item.description: Egyptian coder Assem Sabry has long wanted an AI model that represents his culture. The problem is he hasn’t been able to find one. “The AI industry in Egypt . . . doesn’t exist,” Sabry says. So he built his own: Horus, named after the ancient Egyptian god of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI pulls out of a second Stargate data center deal
item.description: In the space of one week, OpenAI has pulled out of two European Stargate data center deals, one in the UK and the second in Norway.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI shifts its focus to business users amid Anthropic pressure
item.description: OpenAI executives say they will introduce a new artificial intelligence model for “high-value professional work” as the company faces heightened competition with rival Anthropic in attracting corporate customers to adopt AI assistants in their workplaces.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MuleSoft Agent Fabric adds new ways to keep AI agents in line
item.description: Salesforce first sought to tackle AI agent sprawl last year with Agent Fabric, a suite of capabilities and tools inside its MuleSoft AnyPoint Platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Maine to put brakes on big data centers as AI expansion collides with power limits
item.description: Maine is poised to become the first US state to impose a statewide moratorium on large data center construction after lawmakers passed a bill to that effect. The bill needs to be signed by Governor Janet Mills before it can be enacted.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Satellite backhaul service Globalstar has a new, rich owner amid challenging market conditions
item.description: Globalstar, a mobile satellite services (MSS) operator in which Apple has a 20% stake, on Tuesday announced a merger agreement with Amazon, which, pending regulatory approval, could soon bring direct to device services (D2D) services to Leo, the latter’s low Earth orbit satellite network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Curity looks to reinvent IAM with runtime authorization for AI agents
item.description: In 2026, enterprise developers are building and deploying the first generation of powerful, increasingly autonomous AI agents at incredible speed. Now comes the hard part: working out how to secure them. Vendors in the space are facing multiple challenges.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
item.description: Cisco’s move to buy Galileo and—reportedly—Astrix Security is less about filling product gaps and more about rearchitecting how AI is governed, secured, and trusted across the enterprise stack.
item.dc:creator: Network World
